steps.title: Identify the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence claims the ANLA is “known for its impressive audio collection.” The second provides specific evidence: “more than 5,000 audio recordings dating as far back as 1943.” The transition should signal how specific evidence supports a general claim.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “In fact” signals that specific evidence confirms a general claim. The other options misrepresent the relationship: “After” introduces time, “Regardless” introduces concession, and “Instead” introduces an alternative. That matches Choice A — In fact,.
